_meta.note: 12 event cards. One fires per world phase, weighted by scenario, drawn without replacement. Copy is plain language for players with zero context. Real instruments (REDATA, ILO-169) get a one-line gloss; the events themselves are fictional. Effects DSL documented in docs/model-notes.md.

events.name: Tariff threat
events.copy: Washington threatens tariffs on countries that put conditions on tech investment. That includes you.

events.name: Chip export crackdown
events.copy: The US tightens the rules on who can buy advanced chips. Every ally that depends on American computing power gets nervous.

events.name: The data centre offer
events.copy: A tech giant wants to build a huge data centre on your grid. Perfect timing to demand terms — this year, Set conditions works extra hard. (Brazil pioneered a law for exactly this moment, called REDATA.)

events.name: Big Tech vs your courts
events.copy: Your courts take on a major AI company for training its models on local content without paying. For one season, the giants need something from you.

events.name: A summit that works
events.copy: A global AI summit actually goes well for once. Ministers return your calls. Inviting countries into your alliance is cheaper this year.

events.name: China's tech bank
events.copy: China proposes a BRICS technology bank: cheap computing power, friendly financing, familiar faces. Some of your allies are listening. So are you — there is an offer on your table.

events.name: America's compute pact
events.copy: The US floats an all-Americas computing pact: cheap access for countries that play by its rules. Your US-leaning allies are tempted — and there is an offer on your table.

events.name: Minerals boom
events.copy: Mineral prices jump worldwide as AI hardware demand bites. If you set conditions on your minerals, that is bargaining power now. If not, it is just weather.

events.name: The consultation halt
events.copy: A court pauses a big data centre because Indigenous and local communities were never consulted — in much of the world (Brazil included, via ILO-169) that is a real legal duty. Handled badly, this costs public trust.

events.name: The water crisis
events.copy: A data centre's cooling system drains a town's reservoir in the middle of a drought. It is on the news every night, and people are asking who this AI boom is actually for.

events.name: The audit
events.copy: An independent audit asks a simple question: where did the promised local benefits go? If your alliance includes autocracies, the answers look bad this week.

events.name: AI takes a leap
events.copy: AI capability jumps overnight, and nobody outside the superpowers saw it coming. They need everyone else a little less than they did yesterday.
